authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(2) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland streams, rivers, and canals)
sources.quotedText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions. From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish. In waters where the bag limit for trout is zero, trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(152) — Tahoe Lake and tributaries (checked and found NOT to apply: Cathedral Creek is not a direct Lake Tahoe tributary — it feeds Fallen Leaf Lake, which is the 'first lake' on the Taylor Creek drainage, so the closure 'upstream to the first lake' does not reach above Fallen Leaf Lake)
sources.quotedText: (152) Tahoe Lake and tributaries (Placer and El Dorado Cos.). (A) Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout. (B) Tahoe Lake within 300 feet of the mouth of its tributaries. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Cathedral Creek not listed)

CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Cathedral Creek not listed by name; the only potentially relevant entry, (b)(152) Tahoe Lake and tributaries, reaches only tributaries upstream to the first lake and so does not cover this creek above Fallen Leaf Lake)
2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW (Cathedral Creek not listed among El Dorado Co. release waters)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: Fallen Leaf Lake — Wikipedia (establishes Cathedral Creek as a minor feeder of Fallen Leaf Lake, whose only outflow is Taylor Creek into Lake Tahoe)
sources.quotedText: Other much smaller creeks and streams feed the lake, including Cathedral Creek, but they are not nearly as important as Glen Alpine Creek. Taylor Creek is the only outflow, and it is controlled by a long, low concrete dam with an adjustable spillway to control the rate at which the water leaves the lake.

sources.title: OpenStreetMap — Cathedral Creek (way 521703794): a short stream in El Dorado Co. traced from below the Cathedral Lake area (lower Mount Tallac trail) flowing east/northeast into the west side of Fallen Leaf Lake — a tributary of Fallen Leaf Lake, upstream of the 'first lake' on the Taylor Creek drainage
USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 258077, "Cathedral Creek", Stream, CA, El Dorado Co.
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":258077,"gaz_name":"Cathedral Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"El Dorado","objectid_1":1395109,"isunknowncoords":2}, mouth point (Web Mercator -13366246.0060, 4707225.8858 → 38.89908°N, 120.07103°W). Corrects prior file coordinates (-120.08167, 38.89707), which were roughly 950 m southwest of the actual mouth and appear to have been a guessed estimate rather than a GNIS pull; the corrected point is corroborated by the downstream (mouth) endpoint of OpenStreetMap way 521703794, (-120.0709454, 38.8989378), only ~17 m away.

end.verbatim: the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April

parameters.note: Tahoe-tributary check (required for Tahoe-basin waters): Cathedral Creek is within the Lake Tahoe basin but is NOT a direct Lake Tahoe tributary — it is a minor feeder of Fallen Leaf Lake (Wikipedia: 'Other much smaller creeks and streams feed the lake, including Cathedral Creek'), draining east from the Cathedral Lake area on the lower Mount Tallac trail into the west side of Fallen Leaf Lake. Fallen Leaf Lake's only outflow is Taylor Creek, which runs ~2 miles north into Lake Tahoe. The §7.50(b)(152)(A) Tahoe-tributary closure applies only to 'Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake' — on this drainage the first lake is Fallen Leaf Lake, so that closure covers Taylor Creek from Lake Tahoe up to Fallen Leaf Lake but does NOT reach Cathedral Creek, which lies above (upstream of) the first lake. Cathedral Creek is also not listed by its own name in §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide stream regulation §5.85(a)(2): open with a five-trout bag from the last Saturday in April through November 15, then a zero-trout catch-and-release period with gear restrictions from November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April.

regulations.humanSummary: Open the last Saturday in April through November 15 (general statewide stream regulation — not a direct Lake Tahoe tributary, as it lies above Fallen Leaf Lake, so the §7.50(b)(152) Tahoe closure does not apply); November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April is a zero-trout, artificial-lures-with-barbless-hooks period
regulations.verbatimText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions. From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish. In waters where the bag limit for trout is zero, trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland streams, rivers, and canals: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, in effect from the last Saturday in April through November 15.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit, last Saturday in April through November 15
regulations.verbatimText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions.

parameters.counting_rule: Zero-trout bag limit from November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April; trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

regulations.humanSummary: Zero-trout bag limit (catch-and-release for trout), November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April
regulations.verbatimText: From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish. In waters where the bag limit for trout is zero, trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

parameters.note: Gear restriction applies from November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, for all species of fish. During the regular season (last Saturday in April through November 15) the general stream regulation imposes no gear restrictions.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April
regulations.verbatimText: From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. Cathedral Creek is wholly within California (El Dorado Co.); no Nevada reciprocity applies.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
